explanation: Simple query returns only the requested fields for the user.
Querying a non-existent user returns null data with an error entry.
Nested query resolves the user's posts through the relationship resolver.

input: mutation { createUser(input: { name: "Bob", email: "bob@test.com" }) { id name email } }
explanation: CreateUser mutation creates a user and returns the requested fields.

input: mutation { createPost(input: { title: "New Post", content: "Content", authorId: "1" }) { id title author { name } } }
explanation: CreatePost mutation creates a post and resolves the nested author field.
Filtered query returns only posts by the specified author, limited to 5.

input: Query fetching 10 posts with their authors (DataLoader test)
output: 10 posts resolved with only 1 batch query for all unique author IDs
explanation: DataLoader batches individual author lookups into a single batch query, preventing N+1.
Invalid mutation input returns a validation error.
DeletePost mutation removes the post and returns true.

input: query { user(id: "1") { posts { comments { author { posts { title } } } } } }
output: Deeply nested resolution works correctly up to 5 levels
explanation: Nested resolution handles deep chains: user -> posts -> comments -> author -> posts.
